visits.note: Patient P062 is a 55-year-old male with a history of Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Hyperlipidemia. He reports worsening fatigue and shortness of breath over the past month. Physical examination reveals bilateral pitting edema and a blood pressure of 140/90. Labs show HbA1c 7.2, BP 140/90, eGFR 72, and LDL 130. Medications include Metformin 500mg BID, Lisinopril 10mg QD, and Atorvastatin 20mg QD. Diagnoses include Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, Hyperlipidemia, and mild CKD. Symptoms include fatigue, shortness of breath, and pitting edema.

Patient P062 returns 10 months later with worsening shortness of breath and orthopnea. Physical examination reveals a blood pressure of 160/100 and significant bilateral pitting edema. Labs show HbA1c 8.5, BP 160/100, eGFR 55, and LDL 150. Medications are adjusted to include Furosemide 40mg QD for congestive heart failure. Diagnoses include Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, Hyperlipidemia, and moderate CKD. New symptoms include orthopnea and worsening shortness of breath.

Patient P062 returns 6 months later with significant weight gain and worsening edema. Physical examination reveals a blood pressure of 150/90 and significant bilateral pitting edema. Labs show HbA1c 9.0, BP 150/90, eGFR 45, and LDL 140. Medications are adjusted to include Enalapril 10mg QD for heart failure and a referral for dialysis is made. Diagnoses include Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, Hyperlipidemia, and severe CKD. New symptoms include significant weight gain and worsening edema.
